para.name: scope
para.heading: SCOPE
para.text: All agencies must submit to the Board of Estimates (through the Bureau of the Budget and Management Research) all requests for transfer into the following fiscal year of unencumbered operating appropriations existing at the close of the current fiscal year. (Capital project appropriations will be automatically forwarded to the following fiscal year).
All carry-over requests must be received by the Bureau of the Budget and Management Research by the date indicated in the annual letter dealing with this subject from the Budget Chief.

para.name: request-categories
para.heading: REQUEST CATEGORIES
para.text: Requests to carry-over appropriations should be submitted in two categories:

ul.li: To accomplish a purpose for which the funds were originally appropriated.
To accomplish a purpose other than the purpose for which the funds were originally appropriated.

para.name: routine-expenses
para.heading: ROUTINE EXPENSES
para.text: Requests to carry-over appropriations to defray routine expenses will be denied.

para.name: encumbered-items
para.heading: ENCUMBERED ITEMS
para.text: Appropriations for items already encumbered are automatically carried-over; requests are not required.

para.name: availability-of-funds
para.heading: AVAILABILITY OF FUNDS
para.text: Carry-over of appropriations approved by the Board of Estimates is subject to the availability of funds after the posting of all charges applicable to the current fiscal year.

para.name: year-end-accounting-procedure
para.heading: YEAR-END ACCOUNTING PROCEDURE
para.text: To facilitate the closing of the current year's accounts, purchase orders and other contracts received by the Department of Finance after the date indicated in the annual memo from Bureau of Accounting and Management Information Services will be charged against appropriations of the following fiscal year.

para.name: use-of-carry-over-funds
para.heading: USE OF CARRY-OVER FUNDS
para.text: Each carry-over appropriation can only be used for the purpose for which the carry-over was approved by the Board of Estimates.
